Very impressed with the ventilation in the #kubecon public areas; the CO2 is usually in the 450-500 range; 435 in the press room with 10 people in here, which is about the same as being outside. Hope the session and lunch rooms and especially the parties are like that

So Kubecon EU is fucking ENORMOUS. I will report on numbers later but first impressions are just that it's simply very big. The @cncf.io is evidently still a critical foundation (pun intended) for modern software workloads.

I am having so much fun. Kubecon EU > Kubecon NA.

Specifically for my work, it’s bc most of the maintainers are based in the EU, so they can all come.
Just landed at SLC for Kubecon. Where’s everybody at?

I’m sad to be missing #KubeCon this week. It looks like it is going well and I wish I was there!

I am, however, heading up to Vancouver on Thursday for #AtmosphereConf. Looking forward to learning a ton and meeting new people. If you see me please say hi!

Self-service doesn't mean no rules. It means the rules are automated.

Kyverno validates. Crossplane provisions. Secrets flow to pods. Devs get databases in minutes. Platform team sleeps.

Full guide with policy examples:
podostack.com/p/crossplan... 🛠️

For tighter security, add External Secrets Operator.

Crossplane writes creds to Vault. ESO syncs Vault to K8s Secrets. Now credentials rotate automatically and nobody stores them in etcd long-term.

Generate: when a dev creates a DatabaseClaim, Kyverno can auto-create a matching BackupPolicy resource.

No manual step. No Jira ticket. The policy ensures every database gets backups. Zero human intervention.

Validate: block db.r6g.4xlarge Claims. Only allow instance sizes your budget covers.

Mutate: auto-inject billing tags from the team's namespace labels. Devs don't add them. Kyverno does. Every resource tagged. Every time.

Crossplane Claims are K8s resources. That means admission webhooks work on them.

Kyverno validates Claims before they hit Crossplane. Too big? Blocked. Wrong region? Blocked. Missing tags? Auto-added.

Policy as code. Not a wiki page nobody reads.

Giving devs self-service infra without guardrails is like giving them root.

Here's how to prevent $10K RDS instances with one Kyverno policy.

podostack.com/p/crossplan...
